title: Kept on the books, hollowed inside
subtitle: Delhi Public Library: staffing and circulation, 2010-11/2018-19 to 2023-24
source: RTR-WP-006, city-libraries working paper — Delhi Public Library annual reports, 2010-11 to 2023-24.
caption: Delhi Public Library's buildings, board, and collection persist while the service inside them thins. Staff vacancy climbed from 8.5% (2018-19, the best recent low point) to 49.6% (2023-24) — DPL's own annual report says day-to-day tasks are carried out by contract staff for want of sanctioned posts. Circulation, indexed to its 2010-11 level, has fallen to under a quarter: physical loans dropped from 1,169,734 (2010-11) to 274,751 (2023-24). For scale: Toronto Public Library discloses 9.33 borrowings per resident per year; Delhi's is about 0.014 — a gap the paper reads as a disclosure-and-intensity benchmark, not a moral ranking.

data.label: Staff vacancy rate
data.note: 24 vacant posts (2018-19) to 136 (2023-24), of 274 sanctioned. DPL's own annual report: day-to-day tasks run on contract staff for want of sanctioned posts.

data.label: Circulation (index)
data.note: Physical loans/issues, indexed to 2010-11 = 100: 1,169,734 (2010-11) to 274,751 (2023-24) — a real decline, not just a missing-branches problem.
